segments.title: Definition & Scope
segments.theory: Design is the process of creating solutions for visual, functional, or experiential problems. It encompasses creativity, aesthetics, usability, and communication.

questions.question: Which of these best describes design?
Design is only about making things look pretty.

segments.title: History of Design
segments.theory: Design has evolved from ancient craft and architecture to modern digital experiences. Key movements include Bauhaus, Minimalism, and Postmodernism.

questions.question: Which movement focused on simplicity and functionality?

segments.title: Design Disciplines
segments.theory: Design includes graphic design, UI/UX, industrial design, interior design, fashion, and more. Each discipline focuses on solving specific user or aesthetic problems.

questions.question: Which of these is a design discipline?

segments.title: What is Contrast?
segments.theory: Contrast refers to the difference in luminance or color that makes objects distinguishable.

questions.question: Which pair has the highest contrast?

segments.title: Using Tools
segments.theory: Contrast checkers like WCAG tools help you stay accessible.

segments.title: Effective Color Combinations
segments.theory: Understanding complementary, analogous, and triadic color schemes helps create visually appealing and accessible designs.

segments.title: Colors & Emotions
segments.theory: Colors evoke emotional responses. Blue conveys calm, red indicates urgency, and yellow inspires optimism.

questions.question: Which color is often associated with trust?

segments.title: Color in Branding
segments.theory: Brands use colors strategically to influence perception and recognition. For example, green often implies eco-friendliness.

questions.question: Colors have no impact on user perception in branding.

segments.title: Serif vs Sans-Serif
segments.theory: Serif fonts have small strokes at the ends of letters; Sans-Serif fonts are clean and modern. Choice affects readability and tone.

questions.question: Which font type is usually easier to read on screens?

segments.title: Hierarchy & Emphasis
segments.theory: Typography hierarchy guides users through content, highlighting headings, subheadings, and body text appropriately.

questions.question: Using multiple font sizes helps establish a visual hierarchy.

segments.title: Buttons, Inputs & Icons
segments.theory: UI elements must be clear, consistent, and accessible. Use visual cues for interactivity.

questions.question: Which is an essential UI element?

segments.title: User Testing Methods
segments.theory: Conduct surveys, interviews, and usability tests to understand user needs and improve design.

questions.question: Which is a UX research method?

segments.title: Types of Grids
segments.theory: Grids organize content visually. Common types include column, modular, hierarchical, and manuscript grids.

questions.question: Which grid type is often used in newspapers?
